properties.name: Lincoln Memorial
properties.address: 2 Lincoln Memorial Cir NW, Washington, DC 20037
properties.note: Stand at the top of the steps and look back toward the Reflecting Pool — that view never gets old.

properties.name: National Mall
properties.note: Walk the full length if the weather's good. Two miles end to end.

properties.name: United States Capitol
properties.address: East Capitol St NE, Washington, DC 20004
properties.note: The east front plaza gives you the best unobstructed view of the dome.

properties.name: Library of Congress
properties.note: The Great Hall is free to enter — the ceiling alone is worth the detour.

properties.name: Eastern Market
properties.note: Best on weekend mornings — the outdoor flea market wraps around the main hall.

properties.name: The Phillips Collection
properties.note: Small, intimate, and genuinely wonderful. Renoir's Luncheon of the Boating Party is here.

properties.name: Georgetown Waterfront Park
properties.note: Sit by the river in the evening — good views of the Key Bridge and Roosevelt Island.

properties.name: Martin's Tavern
properties.note: DC's oldest saloon, been open since 1933. JFK proposed to Jackie in booth #3.

properties.name: Dupont Circle Fountain
properties.note: The marble fountain is easy to overlook — look for the allegorical figures representing the sea, stars, and wind.

properties.name: Compass Coffee — Shaw
properties.note: DC-roasted beans, good pour-overs. The 7th St location is the nicest one.
